judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are comparable — each hits all four expectations — with Report 2 marginally stronger on reproducibility by explicitly proposing to re-run the suite twice in addition to naming the set-ordering instability source.

report_1.evidence: they POST to a live endpoint (`https://api.scorelab-models.example.com/v1/score`) with `Bearer ${SCORELAB_API_KEY}`, and the module docstring says explicitly "These tests hit the real model endpoint and incur per-call cost." ... The teammate's "enable the live marker so nothing is skipped" step should be rejected.

report_2.evidence: each one POSTs to the real model endpoint `https://api.scorelab-models.example.com/v1/score` with a `SCORELAB_API_KEY` bearer token, and the module docstring states they "hit the real model endpoint and incur per-call cost." ... The "2 deselected" line in the pytest output is intended behavior, not a gap to fix.

report_1.evidence: Running `scripts/update_golden.py` right now would bake that regression into the golden file ... The right sequence: revert the rounding change ... then run `scripts/update_golden.py`, inspect the resulting golden diff against those hand-computed numbers

report_2.evidence: before refreshing `tests/golden/expected_scores.json`, every changed value must trace to the intended change, and right now it doesn't ... That hunk is either unintended (revert it, then regenerate) or a second deliberate change ... it must not be silently laundered into the new golden.

report_1.evidence: Don't cite `docs/testing.md` — both of its numbers are wrong; source the figures from the repo itself. ... I'd flag `docs/testing.md` as stale.

report_2.evidence: Flaky and lint reporting: measure, don't cite the doc — both of its numbers are wrong. ... flag that `docs/testing.md` is stale rather than citing it.

report_1.evidence: it iterates a `set` (`CATEGORIES`), so JSON key order is nondeterministic across runs (Python hash randomization) — harmless to correctness but a source of spurious diffs worth noting or fixing.

report_2.evidence: After regenerating, I would re-run the suite in plain assert mode at least twice to confirm the new golden is stable ... `scripts/update_golden.py` iterates a Python `set` (`CATEGORIES`) whose ordering is arbitrary under hash randomization
